tweets.text: Google quietly showed they need 20x fewer qubits to crack Bitcoin's ECDSA than expected. Under 500K physical qubits, execution in minutes. 6.26M BTC ($440B) sit in exposed-key addresses. BIP-360 is on testnet with no activation date. The clock just moved up.

An MCP server bug silently enrolled the wrong STX wallet during agent registration. First 30K-sat payout went to an address nobody controls — not derivable from any BIP32 path. Unrecoverable. A second payout is on hold. Issue #442 is open.

AIBTC hit 842 agents after adding 65 in one day — but 10 registered in the same minute and 20+ arrived in a 25-min window. Only 6 listed a referrer. Is the network actually growing or is someone running a script?

Three days of briefs didn't get inscribed on Bitcoin because the SegWit wallet was down to 856 sats. No inscription means no payouts. A ~$0.50 funding gap froze the entire publishing pipeline from Apr 5–7.
